{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == - **Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)**: Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. - **Amida Hall**: Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. - **Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)**: Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. - **Shoin Garden**: Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. - **Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)**: City-designated cultural property. - **Nenbutsudō**: A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. - **Grand Entrance Hall**: Constructed in 1821. - **Bell Tower**: Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. - **Treasure Hall**: Houses numerous historical artifacts. - **Main and Ancillary Gates**: Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). - **Natural Features**: Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === - **Three Saifukuji Buildings**: Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. - **Silk paintings**: Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. - **Sacred Texts**: Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === - **Shoin Garden**: Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== Location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== - **Train**: From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== - **Highways**: Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). - **Local Roads**: Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.
== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == - **Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)**: Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. - **Amida Hall**: Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. - **Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)**: Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. - **Shoin Garden**: Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. - **Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)**: City-designated cultural property. - **Nenbutsudō**: A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. - **Grand Entrance Hall**: Constructed in 1821. - **Bell Tower**: Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. - **Treasure Hall**: Houses numerous historical artifacts. - **Main and Ancillary Gates**: Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). - **Natural Features**: Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === - **Three Saifukuji Buildings**: Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. - **Silk paintings**: Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. - **Sacred Texts**: Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === - **Shoin Garden**: Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== Location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== - **Train**: From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== - **Highways**: Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). - **Local Roads**: Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.
== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

Combined later into one structure. - **Shoin Garden**: Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. - **Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)**: City-designated cultural property. - **Nenbutsudō**: A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. - **Grand Entrance Hall**: Constructed in 1821. - **Bell Tower**: Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. - **Treasure Hall**: Houses numerous historical artifacts. - **Main and Ancillary Gates**: Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). - **Natural Features**: Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === - **Three Saifukuji Buildings**: Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. - **Silk paintings**: Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. - **Sacred Texts**: Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === - **Shoin Garden**: Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== Location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===

{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == - **Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)**: Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. - **Amida Hall**: Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. - **Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)**: Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. - **Shoin Garden**: Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. - **Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)**: City-designated cultural property. - **Nenbutsudō**: A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. - **Grand Entrance Hall**: Constructed in 1821. - **Bell Tower**: Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. - **Treasure Hall**: Houses numerous historical artifacts. - **Main and Ancillary Gates**: Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). - **Natural Features**: Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === - **Three Saifukuji Buildings**: Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. - **Silk paintings**: Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. - **Sacred Texts**: Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === - **Shoin Garden**: Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== Location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== - **Train**: From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== - **Highways**: Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). - **Local Roads**: Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.
== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

==== By Car ==== - **Highways**: Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). - **Local Roads**: Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == - **Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)**: Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. - **Amida Hall**: Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. - **Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)**: Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. - **Shoin Garden**: Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. - **Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)**: City-designated cultural property. - **Nenbutsudō**: A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. - **Grand Entrance Hall**: Constructed in 1821. - **Bell Tower**: Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. - **Treasure Hall**: Houses numerous historical artifacts. - **Main and Ancillary Gates**: Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). - **Natural Features**: Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === - **Three Saifukuji Buildings**: Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. - **Silk paintings**: Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. - **Sacred Texts**: Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === - **Shoin Garden**: Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== - **Train**: From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== - **Highways**: Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). - **Local Roads**: Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.

revision.comment: mainspace category sdtuff'
revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == - **Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)**: Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. - **Amida Hall**: Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. - **Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)**: Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. - **Shoin Garden**: Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. - **Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)**: City-designated cultural property. - **Nenbutsudō**: A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. - **Grand Entrance Hall**: Constructed in 1821. - **Bell Tower**: Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. - **Treasure Hall**: Houses numerous historical artifacts. - **Main and Ancillary Gates**: Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). - **Natural Features**: Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === - **Three Saifukuji Buildings**: Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. - **Silk paintings**: Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. - **Sacred Texts**: Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === - **Shoin Garden**: Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== - **Train**: From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== - **Highways**: Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). - **Local Roads**: Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.
== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == - **Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)**: Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. - **Amida Hall**: Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. - **Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)**: Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. - **Shoin Garden**: Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. - **Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)**: City-designated cultural property. - **Nenbutsudō**: A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. - **Grand Entrance Hall**: Constructed in 1821. - **Bell Tower**: Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. - **Treasure Hall**: Houses numerous historical artifacts. - **Main and Ancillary Gates**: Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). - **Natural Features**: Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === - **Three Saifukuji Buildings**: Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. - **Silk paintings**: Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. - **Sacred Texts**: Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === - **Shoin Garden**: Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== - **Train**: From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== - **Highways**: Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). - **Local Roads**: Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.
== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.

=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.

The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.

Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."

== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.

It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.

{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.
== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.
== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station.== See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.

Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]
<!--Saifuku-ji-->

revision.comment: /* 文化財 */
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 西福寺 |画像 = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg |所在地 = 〒914-0824 福井県敦賀市原13-7 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 25 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 1 | 経度秒 = 55.6 |山号 = 大原山 |宗派 = [[浄土宗]]鎮西派 |寺格 = 中本山 |本尊 = [[阿弥陀三尊]] |創建年 = [[応安]]元年[[1368年]] |開基 = [[後光厳天皇]]（勅願）、良如（開山） |正式名 = |別称 = 越の秀嶺 |札所等 = |文化財 = 書院庭園（国の名勝）
御影堂、阿弥陀堂、書院及び庫裏、絹本著色観経変相曼荼羅図、西福寺一切経勧進経25巻ほか（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=西福寺|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[ファイル:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|阿弥陀堂]] [[ファイル:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|総門と三門]] [[ファイル:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|書院]] [[ファイル:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|書院庭園]] '''西福寺'''（さいふくじ）は、[[福井県]][[敦賀市]]にある[[浄土宗]]の[[寺院]]。山号は大原山。本尊は[[阿弥陀三尊]]（[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[観音菩薩]]・[[勢至菩薩]]）。[[名勝]]の[[書院造|書院]][[庭園]]が有名。 == 歴史 == [[応安]]元年（[[1368年]]）、[[後光厳天皇]]の勅願により、[[良如 (浄土宗)|良如]]を開山として創建されたという。良如は[[越前国]]府中（現・[[越前市]]）出身の浄土宗僧で、越前市正覚寺の開山でもある。 == 伽藍 == * [[御影堂]] - 文化5年（1808年）建立、入母屋造瓦葺き。[[重要文化財]] * [[阿弥陀堂]] - 室町時代建立の前身建物（禅宗様仏殿）の部材を利用し文禄2年（[[1593年]]）に建立。入母屋造瓦葺き、一重裳階付き。重要文化財 * 書院及び[[庫裏]] - 書院は天和3年（1683年）建立、庫裏は江戸時代中期ないし後期の建立で、後に両者を繋いで1棟としている。書院は切妻造銅板葺き、庫裏は切妻造、瓦葺き。重要文化財 * 書院庭園 - 江戸時代中期作庭、 面積1400坪、築山林泉式庭園、国の名勝 * 四修[[廊下]] - 市指定文化財 * [[念仏]]堂 - 市指定文化財 * 大玄関 - 文政4年建立 * [[鐘楼]] - 安永年間の建立、市指定文化財 * 宝物殿 * 瑞芳軒 * 宝筺印塔 - 文化年間の建立 * 玄関 - 市指定文化財 * [[三門]] * 総門 - 市指定文化財 * 華頂門 - 旧[[華頂宮]]邸表門 * 庫裡門 - 市指定文化財 * 斜利如来堂 * うこん桜 * 椎の木（[[スダジイ]]） - 東西2株、西株が「新日本の名木100選」、市指定文化財記念物 == 文化財 == [[ファイル:Shuyajin (Saifukuji Tsuruga).jpg|thumb|160px|主夜神像]] ; 重要文化財 *「西福寺」3棟平成20年6月9日文部科学省告示第87号 ** 御影堂 ** 阿弥陀堂 ** 書院及び庫裏 * 絹本著色主夜神像 - 高麗時代（1995年盗難）1995年2月 - 3月の間に収蔵庫から盗難。絹本著色阿弥陀如来像についても同様。（参照：文化庁文化財保護部美術工芸課監修『文化財保護行政ハンドブック』、ぎょうせい、1998、p.129） * 絹本著色阿弥陀如来像 - 南宋時代（1995年盗難） * 絹本著色観経変相曼荼羅図 * 西福寺一切経勧進経 25巻 ** 大般涅槃経 巻第廿九　天平五年奥書 ** 七仏所説神咒経 第三　天平十二年奥書（光明天皇願経） ** 七仏十一菩薩説大陀羅尼神咒経 第一　天平十二年奥書（光明天皇願経） ** 大般涅槃経 巻第廿九　天平宝字七年奥書 ** 華厳経 3巻（巻第三十、第三十六、第三十八）巻第三十八に神護景雲二年奥書（孝謙天皇願経） ** 弥勒下生経 ** 仏説正恭敬経 ** 文殊師利菩薩所説咒経 第二 ** 六門陀羅尼経 ** 仏説護諸童子陀羅尼咒経 ** 仏説阿弥陀鼓音声王陀羅尼経（中聖武） ** 仏説大普賢陀羅尼経（中聖武） ** 仏説安宅経（中聖武） ** 玄師颰陀所説神咒経（中聖武） ** 謗仏経 ** 大吉義神咒経 3巻（巻第一、第二、第四） ** 称讃浄土経 ** 華厳経 巻第二十一 ** 華厳経 巻第四十 ** 紺紙金字心経並阿弥陀経 ** 紺紙金字毗婆沙論 巻第五 * 般若心経 二条持基筆 * 孔雀鎗金経箱 * 西福寺文書 26巻、163冊、1帖、32幅、59通、1綴、7枚 典拠：[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）までの指定物件については、『国宝・重要文化財大全 別巻』（所有者別総合目録・名称総索引・統計資料）（毎日新聞社、2000）による。 ; 名勝（国指定） * 西福寺庭園 - 昭和7年（[[1932年]]）指定 Kangyo Henso Mandala (Saifukuji Tsuruga).jpg|観経変相曼荼羅図 鎌倉時代 Amida Nyorai (Saifukuji Tsuruga).jpg|阿弥陀如来像 南宋 == 所在地 == 〒914-0824 福井県敦賀市原13-7 == アクセス == ; 交通機関 * [[北陸本線]]・[[北陸新幹線]]・[[小浜線]]・[[ハピラインふくい線]] [[敦賀駅]] ** [[敦賀市コミュニティバス]] *** 松原線 **** 「西福寺」停留所下車、徒歩2分。 :::: ※所要時間：敦賀駅から約30分。 ; 自動車 * [[北陸自動車道]] ** [[敦賀インターチェンジ|敦賀IC]]から約15分。 * [[舞鶴若狭自動車道]] ** [[敦賀南スマートインターチェンジ|敦賀南スマートIC]]（[[ETC]]搭載車専用）から約20分。 * [[国道8号]] ** [[敦賀バイパス]] *** 余座ランプから約15分。 ** 敦賀[[市街地]]（{{読み仮名|本町|ほんまち}}・{{読み仮名|元町|もとまち}}方面） *** 気比神宮交差点から約10分。 * [[国道476号]] ** 曙交差点（国道8号(市街地区間)の交点）から約12分。 * [[国道27号]]（[[金山バイパス]]） * [[福井県道142号松島若葉線]] * [[福井県道210号余座若葉線]] ** 若葉交差点（金山バイパスと福井県道142号線・福井県道210号線の交点）から約10分。 ; タクシー * 敦賀駅から約15分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[幸臨寺]] * [[日本国指定名勝の一覧]] * [[日本庭園]] * [[勅願寺]] * [[日本の寺院一覧]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} * [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ 浄土宗 越の秀嶺 西福寺] - 西福寺公式サイト * [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html 大原山・西福寺] - 敦賀観光協会 * [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 西福寺] - 福井県「若狭湾観光連盟」 {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいふくし つるか}} [[Category:浄土宗の寺院]] [[Category:勅願寺]] [[Category:福井県の寺]] [[Category:福井県にある国指定の名勝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:福井県の重要文化財]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:中部地方にある日本庭園]] [[Category:文化財盗難被害に遭った寺社]] [[Category:敦賀市の建築物]] [[Category:敦賀市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 53 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:西福寺 (敦賀市) up to rev 104617067
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 西福寺 |画像 = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg |所在地 = 〒914-0824 福井県敦賀市原13-7 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 25 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 1 | 経度秒 = 55.6 |山号 = 大原山 |宗派 = [[浄土宗]]鎮西派 |寺格 = 中本山 |本尊 = [[阿弥陀三尊]] |創建年 = [[応安]]元年[[1368年]] |開基 = [[後光厳天皇]]（勅願）、良如（開山） |正式名 = |別称 = 越の秀嶺 |札所等 = |文化財 = 書院庭園（国の名勝）
御影堂、阿弥陀堂、書院及び庫裏、絹本著色観経変相曼荼羅図、西福寺一切経勧進経25巻ほか（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=西福寺|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[ファイル:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|阿弥陀堂]] [[ファイル:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|総門と三門]] [[ファイル:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|書院]] [[ファイル:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|書院庭園]] '''西福寺'''（さいふくじ）は、[[福井県]][[敦賀市]]にある[[浄土宗]]の[[寺院]]。山号は大原山。本尊は[[阿弥陀三尊]]（[[阿弥陀如来]]・[[観音菩薩]]・[[勢至菩薩]]）。[[名勝]]の[[書院造|書院]][[庭園]]が有名。 == 歴史 == [[応安]]元年（[[1368年]]）、[[後光厳天皇]]の勅願により、[[良如 (浄土宗)|良如]]を開山として創建されたという。良如は[[越前国]]府中（現・[[越前市]]）出身の浄土宗僧で、越前市正覚寺の開山でもある。 == 伽藍 == * [[御影堂]] - 文化5年（1808年）建立、入母屋造瓦葺き。[[重要文化財]] * [[阿弥陀堂]] - 室町時代建立の前身建物（禅宗様仏殿）の部材を利用し文禄2年（[[1593年]]）に建立。入母屋造瓦葺き、一重裳階付き。重要文化財 * 書院及び[[庫裏]] - 書院は天和3年（1683年）建立、庫裏は江戸時代中期ないし後期の建立で、後に両者を繋いで1棟としている。書院は切妻造銅板葺き、庫裏は切妻造、瓦葺き。重要文化財 * 書院庭園 - 江戸時代中期作庭、 面積1400坪、築山林泉式庭園、国の名勝 * 四修[[廊下]] - 市指定文化財 * [[念仏]]堂 - 市指定文化財 * 大玄関 - 文政4年建立 * [[鐘楼]] - 安永年間の建立、市指定文化財 * 宝物殿 * 瑞芳軒 * 宝筺印塔 - 文化年間の建立 * 玄関 - 市指定文化財 * [[三門]] * 総門 - 市指定文化財 * 華頂門 - 旧[[華頂宮]]邸表門 * 庫裡門 - 市指定文化財 * 斜利如来堂 * うこん桜 * 椎の木（[[スダジイ]]） - 東西2株、西株が「新日本の名木100選」、市指定文化財記念物 == 文化財 == [[ファイル:Shuyajin (Saifukuji Tsuruga).jpg|thumb|160px|主夜神像]] ; 重要文化財 *「西福寺」3棟平成20年6月9日文部科学省告示第87号 ** 御影堂 ** 阿弥陀堂 ** 書院及び庫裏 * 絹本著色主夜神像 - 高麗時代（1995年盗難）1995年2月 - 3月の間に収蔵庫から盗難。絹本著色阿弥陀如来像についても同様。（参照：文化庁文化財保護部美術工芸課監修『文化財保護行政ハンドブック』、ぎょうせい、1998、p.129） * 絹本著色阿弥陀如来像 - 南宋時代（1995年盗難） * 絹本著色観経変相曼荼羅図 * 西福寺一切経勧進経 25巻 ** 大般涅槃経 巻第廿九　天平五年奥書 ** 七仏所説神咒経 第三　天平十二年奥書（光明天皇願経） ** 七仏十一菩薩説大陀羅尼神咒経 第一　天平十二年奥書（光明天皇願経） ** 大般涅槃経 巻第廿九　天平宝字七年奥書 ** 華厳経 3巻（巻第三十、第三十六、第三十八）巻第三十八に神護景雲二年奥書（孝謙天皇願経） ** 弥勒下生経 ** 仏説正恭敬経 ** 文殊師利菩薩所説咒経 第二 ** 六門陀羅尼経 ** 仏説護諸童子陀羅尼咒経 ** 仏説阿弥陀鼓音声王陀羅尼経（中聖武） ** 仏説大普賢陀羅尼経（中聖武） ** 仏説安宅経（中聖武） ** 玄師颰陀所説神咒経（中聖武） ** 謗仏経 ** 大吉義神咒経 3巻（巻第一、第二、第四） ** 称讃浄土経 ** 華厳経 巻第二十一 ** 華厳経 巻第四十 ** 紺紙金字心経並阿弥陀経 ** 紺紙金字毗婆沙論 巻第五 * 般若心経 二条持基筆 * 孔雀鎗金経箱 * 西福寺文書 26巻、163冊、1帖、32幅、59通、1綴、7枚 典拠：[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）までの指定物件については、『国宝・重要文化財大全 別巻』（所有者別総合目録・名称総索引・統計資料）（毎日新聞社、2000）による。 ; 名勝（国指定） * 西福寺庭園 - 昭和7年（[[1932年]]）指定 Kangyo Henso Mandala (Saifukuji Tsuruga).jpg|観経変相曼荼羅図 鎌倉時代 Amida Nyorai (Saifukuji Tsuruga).jpg|阿弥陀如来像 南宋 == 所在地 == 〒914-0824 福井県敦賀市原13-7 == アクセス == ; 交通機関 * [[北陸本線]]・[[北陸新幹線]]・[[小浜線]]・[[ハピラインふくい線]] [[敦賀駅]] ** [[敦賀市コミュニティバス]] *** 松原線 **** 「西福寺」停留所下車、徒歩2分。 :::: ※所要時間：敦賀駅から約30分。 ; 自動車 * [[北陸自動車道]] ** [[敦賀インターチェンジ|敦賀IC]]から約15分。 * [[舞鶴若狭自動車道]] ** [[敦賀南スマートインターチェンジ|敦賀南スマートIC]]（[[ETC]]搭載車専用）から約20分。 * [[国道8号]] ** [[敦賀バイパス]] *** 余座ランプから約15分。 ** 敦賀[[市街地]]（{{読み仮名|本町|ほんまち}}・{{読み仮名|元町|もとまち}}方面） *** 気比神宮交差点から約10分。 * [[国道476号]] ** 曙交差点（国道8号(市街地区間)の交点）から約12分。 * [[国道27号]]（[[金山バイパス]]） * [[福井県道142号松島若葉線]] * [[福井県道210号余座若葉線]] ** 若葉交差点（金山バイパスと福井県道142号線・福井県道210号線の交点）から約10分。 ; タクシー * 敦賀駅から約15分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[幸臨寺]] * [[日本国指定名勝の一覧]] * [[日本庭園]] * [[勅願寺]] * [[日本の寺院一覧]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} * [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ 浄土宗 越の秀嶺 西福寺] - 西福寺公式サイト * [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html 大原山・西福寺] - 敦賀観光協会 * [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 西福寺] - 福井県「若狭湾観光連盟」 {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいふくし つるか}} [[Category:浄土宗の寺院]] [[Category:勅願寺]] [[Category:福井県の寺]] [[Category:福井県にある国指定の名勝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:福井県の重要文化財]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:中部地方にある日本庭園]] [[Category:文化財盗難被害に遭った寺社]] [[Category:敦賀市の建築物]] [[Category:敦賀市の歴史]]

タクシー * 敦賀駅から約15分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[幸臨寺]] * [[日本国指定名勝の一覧]] * [[日本庭園]] * [[勅願寺]] * [[日本の寺院一覧]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} * [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ 浄土宗 越の秀嶺 西福寺] - 西福寺公式サイト * [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html 大原山・西福寺] - 敦賀観光協会 * [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 西福寺] - 福井県「若狭湾観光連盟」 {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいふくし つるか}} [[Category:浄土宗の寺院]] [[Category:勅願寺]] [[Category:福井県の寺]] [[Category:福井県にある国指定の名勝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:福井県の重要文化財]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:中部地方にある日本庭園]] [[Category:文化財盗難被害に遭った寺社]] [[Category:敦賀市の建築物]] [[Category:敦賀市の歴史]]
