revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Saifukuji Tsuruga}}
{{translated page|ja|西福寺 (敦賀市)|version=104617067|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:安土桃山時代の建築
revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period
revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]
{{translated page|ja|西福寺 (敦賀市)|version=104617067|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.

==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

<!--Saifuku-ji--> [[Category:Japanese Gardens in the Chūbu Region]] [[Category:chokugan-ji]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:History of Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:文化財盗難被害に遭った寺社]] [[Category:福井県にある国指定の名勝]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[Tsuruga]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Tsuruga|Tsuruga]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[:en:Tsuruga Station|Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]
{{translated page|ja|西福寺 (敦賀市)|version=104617067|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Saifukuji08s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Jōdo-shū|qid=Q912208}}|prefecture={{ill|Tsuruga|qid=Q847581}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.65699444|N|136.03166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Three Sages of the West|qid=Q10519939}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11629351]] Wikidata name: Saifukuji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Tsuruga|Tsuruga]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[:en:Tsuruga Station|Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Saifukuji08s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208}}|prefecture={{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.65699444|N|136.03166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Three Sages of the West|WD=Q10519939}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629351]] Wikidata name: Saifukuji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Tsuruga|Tsuruga]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[:en:Tsuruga Station|Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Saifukuji08s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208}}|prefecture={{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.65699444|N|136.03166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Three Sages of the West|WD=Q10519939}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629351]] Wikidata name: Saifukuji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Tsuruga|Tsuruga]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[:en:Tsuruga Station|Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Saifukuji08s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208}}|prefecture={{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.65699444|N|136.03166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Three Sages of the West|WD=Q10519939}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629351]] Wikidata name: Saifukuji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) | founder = [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Tsuruga|Tsuruga]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in [[:en:1368|1368]] (Ōan 1) by imperial request from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kōgon|Emperor Go-Kōgon]] and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From [[:en:Tsuruga Station|Tsuruga Station]] (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Saifukuji08s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208}}|prefecture={{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.65699444|N|136.03166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Three Sages of the West|WD=Q10519939}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629351]] Wikidata name: Saifukuji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = {{ill|1368|en|1368|WD=Q6207|lt=1368}} (Ōan 1) | founder = {{ill|Emperor Go-Kōgon|en|Emperor Go-Kōgon|WD=Q555465|lt=Emperor Go-Kōgon}} (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Tsuruga|Tsuruga]], {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture}}. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in {{ill|1368|en|1368|WD=Q6207|lt=1368}} (Ōan 1) by imperial request from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kōgon|en|Emperor Go-Kōgon|WD=Q555465|lt=Emperor Go-Kōgon}} and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station}} (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saifukuji, Tsuruga]][[ja:西福寺 (敦賀市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Saifukuji08s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208}}|prefecture={{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.65699444|N|136.03166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Three Sages of the West|WD=Q10519939}}}} {{Japanese temple infobox | name = Saifukuji | image = Saifukuji08s3200.jpg | location = 13-7 Hara, Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, 914-0824, Japan | latitude = 35 | lat_degrees = 35 | lat_minutes = 39 | lat_seconds = 25 | longitude = 136 | long_degrees = 136 | long_minutes = 1 | long_seconds = 55.6 | mountain = Ōharayama | sect = [[Jōdo-shū]] Chinzei school | temple Rank = Chūhonzan | main image = [[Amida Sanzon]] | founded = {{ill|1368|en|1368|WD=Q6207|lt=1368}} (Ōan 1) | founder = {{ill|Emperor Go-Kōgon|en|Emperor Go-Kōgon|WD=Q555465|lt=Emperor Go-Kōgon}} (imperial request), Ryōnyo (founding priest) | formal name = | other name = Echizen’s Shūrei (秀嶺 of Echizen) | cultural assets = Shoin garden (national scenic spot); Goeidō, Amida Hall, Shoin and Kuri, silk scrolls, and other treasures (important cultural properties) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Saifukuji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
[[File:Saifukuji04s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Amida Hall]] [[File:Saifukuji02s3200.jpg|thumb|200px|Sōmon and Sanmon gates]] [[File:Saifukuji09s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin]] [[File:Saifukuji12s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Shoin Garden]] '''Saifukuji''' (西福寺) is a [[Jōdo-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Tsuruga|Tsuruga]], {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture}}. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Ōharayama. Its principal image is the [[Amida Sanzon]] (Amida Buddha flanked by [[Kannon]] and [[:en:Seishi Bosatsu|Seishi Bosatsu]]). It is renowned for its nationally designated scenic [[Shoin-style]] garden.
== History == Saifukuji was established in {{ill|1368|en|1368|WD=Q6207|lt=1368}} (Ōan 1) by imperial request from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kōgon|en|Emperor Go-Kōgon|WD=Q555465|lt=Emperor Go-Kōgon}} and under the direction of Ryōnyo, a monk from {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}. Ryōnyo, also known as the founder of Shōkakuji Temple in [[Echizen City]], hailed from the same region.
== Temple Layout == * ''''Goeidō (Founder’s Hall)'''': Built in 1808, an important cultural property with ''irimoya''-style roofing. * ''''Amida Hall'''': Constructed in 1593 using parts of a previous Muromachi-period structure. Features ''irimoya''-style roofing with an additional story. * ''''Shoin and Kuri (residence and kitchen)'''': Shoin was constructed in 1683, while the Kuri was built in the mid-Edo period. Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station}} (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Saifukuji, Tsuruga}} - [https://www.saifukuji.jp/ Official Website] - [https://www.turuga.org/places/saifukuji/saifukuji.html Saifukuji - Tsuruga Tourism Association] - [https://wakasabay.jp/list/detail?genre=spot&id=5d6f6b4b7765619cb2230300 Saifukuji - Wakasa Bay Tourism]
{{translated page|ja|西福寺 (敦賀市)|version=104617067|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Combined later into one structure. * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Created in the mid-Edo period, this 1400-tsubo garden features a ''tsukiyama'' (hill-and-pond) design. * ''''Four Corridors (Shūshū Rōka)'''': City-designated cultural property. * ''''Nenbutsudō'''': A hall for chanting nenbutsu, also a city-designated cultural property. * ''''Grand Entrance Hall'''': Constructed in 1821. * ''''Bell Tower'''': Built during the An'ei era, designated as a cultural property. * ''''Treasure Hall'''': Houses numerous historical artifacts. * ''''Main and Ancillary Gates'''': Including Sōmon, Sanmon, and Kachōmon (a gate from the former Kachō Palace). * ''''Natural Features'''': Notable trees such as Ukon cherry blossoms and two ancient Japanese chinquapin trees (''Quercus cuspidata''), with one designated among "100 Famous Trees of Japan."
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Important Cultural Properties === * ''''Three Saifukuji Buildings'''': Goeidō, Amida Hall, and Shoin/Kuri complex. * ''''Silk paintings'''': Including depictions of Amida Buddha and the Kangyō Mandala. * ''''Sacred Texts'''': Manuscripts dating to the Nara and Heian periods, including illuminated sutras.
=== National Scenic Spot === * ''''Shoin Garden'''': Recognized in 1932 for its exceptional Edo-period design.
=== Stolen Artifacts === - Two important cultural property scrolls were stolen in 1995, including a depiction of Shuyajin and an Amida Buddha from the Southern Song dynasty.
=== location === 〒914-0824 Fukui Prefecture, Tsuruga City, Hara 13-7
=== Access ===
==== Public Transit ==== * ''''Train'''': From {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station|1=Tsuruga Station}} (JR Hokuriku, Obama, and Hokuriku Shinkansen lines), take the Tsuruga Community Bus (Matsubara Line) to the "Saifukuji" stop, a 2-minute walk from the temple.
==== By Car ==== * ''''Highways'''': Approx. 15 minutes from [[Tsuruga IC]] (Hokuriku Expressway) or 20 minutes from [[Tsuruga South Smart IC]] (Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway). * ''''Local Roads'''': Accessible via National Routes 8, 27, or 476.
==== Taxi ==== - About 15 minutes from Tsuruga Station. == See Also == - [[Kōrinji]] - [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan]] - [[:en:Japanese gardens|Japanese gardens]] - [[Imperial temples]] - [[List of Japanese temples]]
